Read result for supabase/migrations/001_init.sql
Read Content:
Lines 496-530:
496→-- Members can request to join a gym (INSERT their own row, status defaults to 'pending')
497→CREATE POLICY "gym_members: member can request join"
498→  ON public.gym_members FOR INSERT
499→  WITH CHECK (
500→    user_id = auth.uid()
501→    AND status = 'pending'
502→    -- Only 'member' role users can request membership
503→    AND (SELECT role FROM public.users WHERE id = auth.uid()) = 'member'
504→  );
505→
506→-- Gym admin can approve/reject/update members of their gym
507→CREATE POLICY "gym_members: gym admin manages their members"
508→  ON public.gym_members FOR UPDATE
509→  USING (public.is_gym_admin_of(gym_id))
510→  WITH CHECK (public.is_gym_admin_of(gym_id));
511→
512→-- Super Admin can update any membership
513→CREATE POLICY "gym_members: super admin updates any"
514→  ON public.gym_members FOR UPDATE
515→  USING (public.is_super_admin());
516→
517→-- Members can withdraw their own pending request
518→CREATE POLICY "gym_members: member deletes own pending request"
519→  ON public.gym_members FOR DELETE
520→  USING (user_id = auth.uid() AND status = 'pending');
521→
522→
523→-- ----------------------------------------
524→-- RLS: qr_tokens
525→-- ----------------------------------------
526→-- CRITICAL: Members NEVER write to this table. Server (service role) only.
527→
528→-- Gym admin can VIEW their gym's current active token (to display QR)
529→CREATE POLICY "qr_tokens: gym admin reads own gym token"
530→  ON public.qr_tokens FOR SELECT
EOF

112→-- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
113→-- TABLE: gym_members
114→-- Junction table: User ↔ Gym relationship.
115→-- A member can belong to multiple gyms. Each membership is independent.
116→-- `approved_by` = the gym_admin who approved the request.
117→-- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
118→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.gym_members (
119→  id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
120→  gym_id              UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.gyms(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
121→  user_id             UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
122→  status              membership_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
123→  -- Subscription tracking
124→  subscription_plan   subscription_plan,
125→  subscription_status subscription_status DEFAULT 'trial',
126→  subscription_start  DATE,
127→  subscription_end    DATE,
128→  amount_paid         NUMERIC(10, 2) DEFAULT 0,
129→  -- Notes & admin
130→  notes               TEXT,
131→  approved_by         UUID REFERENCES public.users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
132→  approved_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
133→  joined_at           TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
134→  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
135→  updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
136→  -- A user can only have one active membership record per gym
137→  UNIQUE (gym_id, user_id)
138→);
139→
140→COMMENT ON TABLE public.gym_members IS 'M:N junction: user <-> gym. Controls B2B access (check-in, gym features).';
141→
142→
143→-- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
144→-- TABLE: qr_tokens
145→-- Server-generated rotating tokens for gym check-in.
EOF

742→-- Auto-create a row in `public.users` when a new Supabase Auth user signs up.
743→-- Pulls email and full_name from auth.users metadata.
744→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_auth_user()
745→RETURNS TRIGGER
746→LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
747→SET search_path = public
748→AS $$
749→BEGIN
750→  INSERT INTO public.users (id, email, full_name, role)
751→  VALUES (
752→    NEW.id,
753→    NEW.email,
754→    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name', 'New User'),
755→    'member'  -- All signups default to member. Role is elevated manually.
756→  );
757→  RETURN NEW;
758→END;
759→$$;
760→
761→CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER trg_on_auth_user_created
762→  AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
763→  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.handle_new_auth_user();
764→
765→
766→-- Update gym member count when a member is approved or removed
767→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.sync_gym_member_count()
768→RETURNS TRIGGER
769→LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
770→AS $$
EOF

1→-- Add phone column to users table
2→ALTER TABLE public.users
3→ADD COLUMN phone VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE;
4→
5→-- Create index for phone lookups
6→CREATE INDEX idx_users_phone ON public.users(phone);
7→
8→-- Update auth trigger to sync phone from user metadata
9→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
10→RETURNS trigger AS $$
11→BEGIN
12→  INSERT INTO public.users (id, email, full_name, phone, role)
13→  VALUES (
14→    NEW.id,
15→    NEW.email,
16→    NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name',
17→    NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'phone',
18→    'member'
19→  )
20→  ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE
21→  SET
22→    email = EXCLUDED.email,
23→    full_name = EXCLUDED.full_name,
24→    phone = EXCLUDED.phone;
25→  RETURN NEW;
26→END;
27→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
EOF
